blocks.goal: Move all array-based read/write helpers and their helper validateByteArrayOffset out of EndianUtils into a dedicated EndianUtilsArray class to group array-related functionality and reduce the public surface of EndianUtils.

ops.details: Create a new class EndianUtilsArray in package org.apache.commons.io. Move the array-based public static read/write methods and the private helper validateByteArrayOffset into this class. Preserve the original implementations (including the same try-catch behavior that throws IllegalArgumentException on IndexOutOfBoundsException). Ensure EndianUtilsArray keeps references to EndianUtilsSwap and EndianUtilsStream as required by the implementations. Make methods public static so callers can use them directly.

Move the method bodies from EndianUtils into EndianUtilsArray. After this step EndianUtilsArray will contain the full implementations for array-based operations and the validator. Keep EndianUtils methods unchanged for now (they will be updated in a later step) to avoid breaking the build while new class appears.

Update related tests affected by this refactoring block so the configured build command passes. Adjust imports, package references, moved class references, constructors, and assertions only when required. Do not change production behavior through tests.

blocks.goal: Move all InputStream/OutputStream based read/write helpers into a dedicated EndianUtilsStream class to group stream-related functionality and reduce EndianUtils responsibility.

ops.details: Create a new class EndianUtilsStream in package org.apache.commons.io. Move the stream-based public static read/write methods into this class preserving signatures and behavior. Ensure methods declare the same IOExceptions as in the original EndianUtils methods. Keep implementations identical to preserve behavior.

Move the method bodies from EndianUtils into EndianUtilsStream. After this step EndianUtilsStream will contain the stream-handling implementations. Leave EndianUtils wrappers intact for now to maintain compilation while making the new class available.

Update related tests affected by this refactoring block so the configured build command passes. Adjust imports, package references, moved class references, constructors, and assertions only when required. Do not change production behavior through tests.

blocks.goal: Move all byte-swapping helper methods into EndianUtilsSwap to centralize low-level bitwise conversion logic and further shrink EndianUtils.

ops.details: Move the swap* method bodies from EndianUtils into EndianUtilsSwap. Ensure EndianUtilsSwap has public static methods with the same signatures. Adjust implementations if they previously referenced private helpers now moved to other classes. Keep semantics identical. Note: EndianUtils already references EndianUtilsSwap; this consolidates swap logic in that class.

Update related tests affected by this refactoring block so the configured build command passes. Adjust imports, package references, moved class references, constructors, and assertions only when required. Do not change production behavior through tests.

blocks.goal: Update call sites across the project so callers use the specialized EndianUtilsArray, EndianUtilsStream, and EndianUtilsSwap classes instead of the large EndianUtils class, and reduce EndianUtils to a minimal facade.
files: all project source files that reference EndianUtils

ops.details: Replace calls that targeted array-based EndianUtils methods with calls to EndianUtilsArray methods. Update imports where necessary to reference org.apache.commons.io.EndianUtilsArray. This directs array-specific calls to the new specialized class.
Replace calls that targeted stream-based EndianUtils methods with calls to EndianUtilsStream methods. Update imports to reference org.apache.commons.io.EndianUtilsStream.
Replace calls that targeted swap* methods on EndianUtils with calls to EndianUtilsSwap. Update imports accordingly.

inputs: source files updated in previous ops
outputs: source files with corrected imports for EndianUtilsArray, EndianUtilsStream, EndianUtilsSwap
ops.details: Where call sites were updated, add or adjust imports to reference the new classes in org.apache.commons.io. Remove unused EndianUtils imports where applicable.

Replace the large EndianUtils implementation with a minimal facade that documents and delegates to the specialized classes for compatibility where desired. The new EndianUtils will be a small class (same package) that either (a) exposes a very small curated subset of convenience methods delegating internally to EndianUtilsArray/EndianUtilsStream/EndianUtilsSwap, or (b) is left as a formally deprecated holder with only the deprecated constructor and Javadoc, advising callers to use the specialized classes directly. This reduces the public method count on EndianUtils and centralizes responsibilities.

Update related tests affected by this refactoring block so the configured build command passes. Adjust imports, package references, moved class references, constructors, and assertions only when required. Do not change production behavior through tests.

blocks.goal: Safety and consistency passes: ensure no duplicate behavior remains and update any internal references or package-private helpers that were moved.

outputs: these files with correct imports (java.io.InputStream, java.io.OutputStream, java.io.IOException, static IOUtils.EOF if used)
ops.details: Ensure each new/modified file contains the necessary imports preserved from the original EndianUtils (e.g., java.io.InputStream, java.io.OutputStream, java.io.IOException, and static import of IOUtils.EOF if used). This ensures each block compiles after extraction.

inputs: internal references inside moved methods that referenced private helpers in EndianUtils
outputs: references adjusted to use EndianUtilsArray/EndianUtilsStream/EndianUtilsSwap as appropriate
ops.details: Adjust any internal references (for example private validator) so moved methods reference the helpers in their new host class. If a helper was shared and necessary in multiple classes, duplicate it conservatively or consider making it package-private in the appropriate class.

Update related tests affected by this refactoring block so the configured build command passes. Adjust imports, package references, moved class references, constructors, and assertions only when required. Do not change production behavior through tests.
